iteration_history.description: EN-1 encoding fix: removed Photoperiod_LD and Light from exogenous_supply in 30 LD gene-KO tests, restoring WT comparison baseline by defusing the validator's auto-'mutant' override.
EN-2 extended LD-encoding fix to T030 (Gibberellin treatment) + STRUCT-1 added direct ID1 -> Flowering_Time(-1) edge to capture sbid1-2 170-day delay masked by cascade dilution.

EN-3 SD-baseline epistasis encoding: added comparison_baseline='epistasis' with network_baseline_modifiers={Photoperiod_LD:0.0} to all 8 SD-condition tests so that WT-under-SD is the comparison instead of WT-under-LD.

fixes_applied.description: Removed Photoperiod_LD and Light from exogenous_supply in 30 LD gene-KO tests (T001, T003-T007, T009, T011, T013, T014, T031, T033, T038-T040, T044, T045, T047, T049, T050, T062, T063, T065, T068, T069, T072, T075, T078, T079, T082).
fixes_applied.reason: Validation_common.py lines 281-286 override comparison_baseline to 'mutant' whenever both gene_modifiers (any value != 1.0) AND exogenous_supply are present. The LD encoding convention (exogenous={Photoperiod_LD:1, Light:1}) unintentionally triggered this rescue-comparison for every LD gene-KO test. The intended comparison is mutant-under-LD vs WT-under-LD; since source nodes already default to 1.0 (LD-permissive), the exogenous boost was both double-counting LD and triggering the rescue override.
fixes_applied.biological_justification: Under the v2.0 source-node convention, source value = gene_modifier (default 1.0) + exogenous_supply (default 0.0). WT baseline with sources=1.0 already represents permissive LD-like conditions, which is biologically appropriate for sorghum (facultative SD plant with LD repression of flowering). Removing LD/Light exogenous leaves LD/Light at the default 1.0 (LD-permissive) and restores correct WT comparison.

fixes_applied.description: Extended EN-1 fix to T030 (Gibberellin treatment, empty gene_modifiers but LD/Light present). Removed LD/Light from T030's exogenous_supply, keeping Gibberellin=1.0.
fixes_applied.reason: T030 failed in iter 1 with ratio=10 (K-cap) because Photoperiod_LD=2.0 + Light=2.0 (1.0 default + 1.0 exogenous) propagated through the cascade, saturating Flowering_Time to the ceiling. Stripping LD/Light isolates the Gibberellin signal and predicts ratio=0.5 ('decreased') as expected.
fixes_applied.biological_justification: Gibberellin treatment experiments occur under natural LD; algebraic WT baseline already captures LD-permissive conditions. Double-counting LD produces non-physiological source saturation.

fixes_applied.description: Added direct ID1 -> Flowering_Time edge with sign=-1 (ID1 reduces days-to-flower); edge_id N081.
fixes_applied.reason: T016/T017/T018 SbID1 LOF predicted strong 'decreased' (ratio=0.0024) when biology says sbid1-2 delays flowering ~170 days (strong 'increased'). Diagnosis: ID1 KO lowers both florigen activators AND florigen repressors (PRR37/GHD7/ELF3/etc.); bounded-inverse inhibition saturates at K=10 and partially rescues FT values. Inhibitor product stays large (~412), inhibition stays small, Flowering_Time low -> wrong direction. The direct edge puts ID1 into the Flowering_Time inhibitor product; ID1=0 -> product=0 -> floor 0.1 -> cap 10 -> Flowering_Time=10 -> 'increased'. For every other test (ID1=1.0 default) the edge is multiplicative identity and has no effect.
fixes_applied.biological_justification: SbID1 (Gladman 2023) is the single strongest-effect flowering-time gene in sorghum (sbid1-2 Q199* delays flowering ~170 days). Gladman shows direct ID1 dependence of SbCO, SbCN8, SbCN12 florigen transcripts. The short-cut edge collapses the multipath integrative effect into a single framework-tractable edge that captures the empirical delay that geometric-mean dilution masks. This is a modeling short-cut per REFINEMENT_AGENT Section 1.5 Step C, not a novel biological claim.

fixes_applied.description: Added comparison_baseline='epistasis' with network_baseline_modifiers={Photoperiod_LD: 0.0} to 8 SD-condition tests (T002, T010, T015, T055, T056, T073, T076, T077). Validator now compares mutant-under-SD against WT-under-SD rather than default WT-under-LD-permissive baseline.
fixes_applied.reason: SD tests use gene_modifier={Photoperiod_LD:0} to represent short-day. Previously the baseline was WT-under-LD (Photoperiod_LD=1), mixing two changes (photoperiod OFF + gene KO) in one ratio. That produced strong 'decreased' when biology said 'unchanged' (T010, T076, T077) or 'increased' (T015). Epistasis baseline with Photoperiod_LD=0 isolates the gene-KO effect: both baseline and perturbed cascades have LD=0, the cascade collapse is common-mode and cancels, the residual gene-KO effect matches biology.
fixes_applied.biological_justification: Paper-reported SD effects are always relative to SD controls in the same experiment. Encoding the SD baseline as WT-under-SD aligns the algebraic comparison with the underlying experimental design.

framework_limitations.description: PHOTOPERIOD continuous_dark (Light=0, Photoperiod_LD=0): entire network cascade crashes when Light=0 because all photoperiodic activators floor at 0.01. Model predicts strong 'decreased'; biology expects 'unchanged' (dark-grown sorghum does not flower, but days-to-flowering is not well defined). Framework cannot model the empirical definition ambiguity.

SbCCA1 GOF expected 'increased' (CCA1 OE delays flowering via clock disruption). Algebraic model predicts 'decreased' because CCA1 directly represses PRR37 (strong edge) and CCA1 OE amplifies PRR37 repression -> EHD1 release -> FT up -> Flowering_Time down. Whole-oscillator disruption from CCA1 OE cannot be represented in a steady-state algebraic framework with a single CCA1 phase.

SbGI OE LD expected 'increased' (delays flowering via PRR37 amplification). Model predicts 'decreased' with ratio=0.92 (borderline threshold). GI's geometric-mean contribution to PRR37 (1/6th weight) is too small to propagate the expected delay; deepening the GI->PRR37 coupling would require non-default weights not supported by the framework.

SbID1 rescue experiment (ID1=1.0 + exogenous florigen?). Framework adds exogenous supply regardless of whether signaling is functional; classic Trap 5 signaling-mutant rescue limit. fixable=false per failure_analysis.

SbELF3 LOF LD expected 'decreased' (per Zhao 2012 oself3 rice under LD). Model predicts 'increased' because ELF3 represses PRR37/GHD7; ELF3 loss releases PRR37/GHD7 -> FT down -> Flowering_Time up. The rice paper's 'accelerated flowering' phenotype under LD involves phase-dependent ELF3 effects (ELF3 acts both as repressor AND in clock phase gating) that a steady-state algebraic model with a single sign-encoded edge cannot capture.

SbPRR37 SbCO epistasis expected 'decreased'. Framework cannot isolate the CO activation arm from the PRR37 repression arm when both are perturbed; bounded-inverse cap dominates. fixable=false per failure_analysis.

Ma2_SMYD LOF SD expected 'unchanged'. After EN-3 epistasis fix, algebraic predicts 'increased' (ratio=23.6) because loss of Ma2 under SD still derepresses the cascade further than WT-under-SD. A tighter cascade-collapse encoding would be needed.
SbELF3 LOF LD expected 'decreased' (Tadesse 2024 DOI). Same framework limit as T068 - phase-gated ELF3 cannot be represented by a single sign edge. fixable=false.

LD.note: No change in LD headline after iter 1 - iter 1 EN-1 fix already saturated LD gains.

SD.note: Iter 3 EN-3 encoding fix delivered all SD improvements.

both.note: Iter 2 ID1 edge restored 'both' ALG to 1.0.

continuous_dark.note: Framework limit - Light=0 crashes cascade, cannot model empirical 'unchanged' phenotype.

PRR37_LOF.note: Iter 1 EN-1 encoding fix alone brought this class from 0% (0/6) to 100% (6/6).

PhyC_LOF.note: Partial: T010 SbPhyC SD still fails on algebraic/RWR due to framework SD-cascade-collapse.

MA2_SMYD.note: Iter 1 fixed T011 (LD). T077 SD residually fails as framework limit.

ID1_LOF.note: Iter 2 STRUCT-1 ID1 -> Flowering_Time edge fixed T016/T017/T018 on algebraic and RWR.

SbFT_CRISPR.note: Iter 1 EN-1 fixed T013/T038. T056 still partial on ODE.

clock (ELF3/GI/CCA1).note: Mostly unchanged: T049 CCA1 OE, T068/T079 ELF3 LOF flagged as framework limits.

photoperiod_only.note: T026 continuous_dark remains a framework limit.

notes: Refinement delivered a +22.1% best-method gain (RWR 64.9 -> 87.0). All three methods landed in substantial-kappa territory (alg kappa=0.79, ode 0.73, rwr 0.77). FRS/DARS moved from 'small-scale solid' (5.08) to 'large-scale strong' (10.37) - a clean two-band promotion. Major mechanism-level wins: PRR37_LOF 0% -> 100%, PhyB_LOF 25% -> 100%, Ghd7_LOF 0% -> 100%, ID1_LOF 0% -> 100%, SbFT CRISPR 50% -> 75%. Residual 9-12 failures per method are distributed across framework limits (continuous_dark, ELF3 phase-gating, CCA1 clock disruption, T064 rescue, T072 epistasis) and borderline threshold cases (T050 SbGI OE ratio 0.92). Total structural change: 1 edge added (ID1 -> Flowering_Time, DOI 10.3389/fpls.2023.1304822); 4 encoding fixes (EN-1, EN-2, EN-3 all targeting the Photoperiod_LD/Light exogenous convention and the SD comparison-baseline convention).
